tool_args.label: Minutes of the Regular Meeting Joint LIRR and Metro-North Committees - September 23, 2024

payload.title: Minutes of the Regular Meeting Joint Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad Committees

evidence_refs.source_quote: Minutes of the Regular Meeting Joint Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad Committees Monday, September 23, 2024 10:45 a.m.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Two-year total renovation of Babylon Station including two new 12-car platforms with automated snow and ice melt technology; new canopies, stairs, handrails, escalators and ADA-compliant elevators

evidence_refs.source_quote: broke ground on the two-year total renovation of Babylon Station

payload.description: Ongoing state of good repair project for Metro-North Railroad

evidence_refs.source_quote: President Rinaldi highlighted the ongoing Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement project, which is an important state of good repair project for the railroad.

payload.description: Proposed $6 billion LIRR allocation and $6 billion Metro-North allocation for state of good repair, fleet replacement, and expansion projects

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA C&D President Jamie Torres-Springer presented the proposed Capital Plan for 2025-2029

payload.description: Proposed capital plan including $6 billion LIRR allocation and $6 billion Metro-North allocation for state of good repair, fleet replacement, and expansion projects

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA C&D President Jamie Torres-Springer (“President Torres-Springer”) presented the proposed Capital Plan for 2025-2029, which was released the previous week.

payload.description: Next phase of Jamaica Capacity Improvements, part of long-term strategy to modernize tracks through Jamaica and eliminate the 'Jamaica crawl'

evidence_refs.source_quote: This plan will implement the next phase of Jamaica Capacity Improvements, part of the long-term strategy to modernize the tracks that run through Jamaica and eliminate the “Jamaica crawl”.
Metro-North served 5.45 million customers, which is a 6.5% increase compared to August 2023 and represented 74.7% of August 2019 ridership.

tool_args.label: Metro-North August 2024 weekend ridership vs August 2019 - 83.5%

evidence_refs.source_quote: Average weekday ridership decreased but average weekend ridership remained strong at 83.5% of August 2019.
Average weekday ridership rose to a record 224,932; weekday ridership stood at 78.0% of September 2019.
weekday ridership stood at 78.0% of September 2019
Tuesday-Thursday ridership rose similarly to a new record of 230,176, the first time this metric has topped 230,000
Tue-Thu ridership... at approximately 79.8% of September 2019

payload.raw_value_text: up 5% over the previous post-COVID record weekend of June 8-9

evidence_refs.source_quote: up 5% over the previous post-COVID record weekend of June 8-9
Tuesday-Thursday ridership rose similarly to a new record of 230,176, the first time this metric has topped 230,000, at approximately 79.8% of September 2019
Metro-North's service-delivered rate for August was 99.8%.
Systemwide on-time performance (OTP) for August was 97.9%, above goal of 94%, while year-to-date OTP was also above goal at 98.3%.
year-to-date OTP was also above goal at 98.3%
Mean distance between failures (MDBF) for the fleet was 354,788 miles in July 2024, above the goal of 200,000 miles.
July ridership increased by 14.9% and August ridership increased by 12% over the same months in 2023.
August ridership increased by 12% over the same months in 2023
Year-to-date (“YTD”) ridership ending in August is 16.3% above 2023 YTD ridership
monthly commutation ridership increased 18.9% compared to 2023
LIRR broke the one-day, post-COVID ridership mark on Wednesday, September 18 with 281,624 customers
on-time performance (“OTP”) was 95.6% for July and 94.5% for August
on-time performance (“OTP”) was 95.6% for July and 94.5% for August
YTD OTP was 95.5%, all above goal
The mean distance between failure (“MDBF”) for July was 166,391 miles, and YTD MDBF was 160,347 miles, both above goal of 150,000 miles.
YTD MDBF was 160,347 miles
Service delivery for August was 99.6% of trips completed.

payload.raw_value_text: surpassing last year's tournament ridership by a healthy 24%

tool_args.label: MTAPD August 2024 major felonies - 20 total, 17% decrease

payload.raw_value_text: twenty incidents of major felonies for both railroads combined, ten for each railroad, representing a 17% decrease

evidence_refs.source_quote: crime for the month of August remains low at only twenty incidents of major felonies for both railroads combined, ten for each railroad, representing a 17% decrease as compared to last year.

tool_args.label: MTAPD fare dispute calls increase - over 300%

payload.raw_value_text: over 300% of calls relating to fare disputes, from an average of 300 calls per month to nearly 1200 calls a month

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTAPD has experienced a significant increase of over 300% of calls relating to fare disputes, from an average of 300 calls per month to nearly 1200 calls a month.

payload.description: Groundbreaking ceremony for the two-year total renovation of Babylon Station

evidence_refs.source_quote: On September 20, President Free joined Construction and Development (“C&D”) President Jamie Torres-Springer, Co-chair Herbst, and SMART Transportation Division General Chairman Anthony Simon as they broke ground on the two-year total renovation of Babylon Station.

payload.description: Metro-North fall schedule change on Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven lines

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North's fall schedule change on the Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven lines will be in effect beginning Sunday, October 6

payload.description: New platform at Babylon Station scheduled to open

evidence_refs.source_quote: The new platform is scheduled to open in May 2026.

proposed $6 billion LIRR Capital Plan

payload.raw_value_text: $800 million allocation to be used across all agencies to advance promising expansion of capacity upgrade projects

tool_args.label: Waterbury Branch out of service after Aug 18 storm

payload.claim_text: The Waterbury Branch sustained the most damage and will remain out of service for the next several weeks.
payload.subject: Waterbury Branch
payload.description: Waterbury Branch out of service due to storm damage from August 18 storm

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Waterbury Branch sustained the most damage and will remain out of service for the next several weeks.

payload.claim_text: New pilot program using QR codes on posters in select restrooms on trains and in stations to allow customers to provide real-time feedback on restroom cleanliness.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

evidence_refs.source_quote: President Rinaldi introduced a new pilot program to improve restroom cleanliness. QR codes on posters in select restrooms on trains and in stations will allow customers to provide real-time feedback on their experience.

payload.claim_text: Metro-North will begin restoration of track bed and signals in third week of October, service estimated to resume by early November. Full-schedule substitute bus service is operating for Waterbury Branch customers.
payload.subject: Waterbury Branch

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North will begin restoration of the track bed and signals in the third week of October, with service estimated to resume by early November. In the meantime, Metro-North is operating a full-schedule substitute bus service for the Waterbury Branch customers.

tool_args.label: External assessments of needed capital spending

payload.claim_text: JP Morgan assessment found annual capital expenditures of $16-$23 billion necessary to match comparable peers. NYS Comptroller independently estimated total capital needed of $57.8 to $92.2 billion for 2025-2029 Capital Program.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Plan

evidence_refs.source_quote: an assessment by JP Morgan found that annual capital expenditures of $16-$23 billion were necessary to match comparable peers. The NYS Comptroller independently estimated that total capital needed to be in the range of $57.8 to $92.2 billion for the 2025-2029 Capital Program.

tool_args.label: LIRR drone program for right-of-way monitoring

payload.claim_text: LIRR has identified a more efficient way to monitor and identify issues along the right of way through the use of drones, operated by the Office of Security.
payload.subject: Long Island Rail Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: The LIRR has identified a more efficient way to monitor and identify issues along the right of way through the use of drones.

tool_args.label: Capital Plan Metro-North focus - state of good repair

payload.claim_text: For Metro-North, the Capital Plan yields a six billion dollar allocation that primarily focuses on state of good repair.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the Capital Plan yields a six billion dollar allocation that primarily focuses on state of good repair
